title: Compiled the surrogacy research foundation and competitor analysis
purpose: Assemble reference material and market analysis to guide the clinic website's messaging and structure.
processSummary: Captured competitor pages and reference materials into the research repo, then synthesized them into a long-form report focused on UAE-market positioning, messaging gaps, and landing-page structure.
outcome: Zaya Life gained a dedicated research base and a synthesized copy brief that could feed both the site and future positioning work.

title: Refined the clinic website's brand, tone, and parent-facing copy
purpose: Make the initial surrogacy site feel more trustworthy, less corporate, and better aligned with intended parents.
processSummary: Updated naming and logo usage, softened the font and tone, rewrote the homepage and parent-facing copy to remove choppy phrases and heavy legalistic language, and removed price references for a more careful early presentation.
outcome: The Zaya Life site moved from a rough initial draft toward a more emotionally calibrated and parent-friendly marketing surface.

title: Productionized the intake and polished the site layout
purpose: Convert the marketing site from a static draft into something closer to a usable intake surface.
processSummary: Connected the intake flow through Formspree, added validation for email and phone fields, repaired CTA behavior, added WhatsApp contact, iterated on images for parent and program pages, and ran a responsiveness pass with follow-up layout fixes.
outcome: The site became much more usable: intake submission was wired, navigation behaved properly, and the main marketing pages were adapted for better mobile and desktop presentation.
